name: Structure Therapeutics Inc.
business: Structure Therapeutics Inc. is a clinical stage global biopharmaceutical company developing novel oral small molecule therapeutics to treat chronic diseases, primarily obesity and related conditions, by targeting G-protein coupled receptors (GPCRs). The company's most advanced product candidate is aleniglipron, an oral small molecule GLP-1R agonist for obesity, and it also has oral small molecule amylin receptor agonists and other pipeline programs in development.

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical stage global biopharmaceutical company developing novel oral small molecule therapeutics to treat a wide range of chronic diseases with unmet medical need.

catalysts.event: Report topline 44-week data from ACCESS II study
catalysts.sentence: Topline 44-week data from the ACCESS II study are expected in the first quarter of 2026.
The Company anticipates initiating the Phase 3 program in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Report topline results from maintenance switching study
catalysts.sentence: the Company expects to report topline results from its maintenance switching study evaluating the transition from an approved injectable GLP1-RA injectable to aleniglipron for weight loss maintenance, its Phase 2 randomized placebo controlled study assessing aleniglipron at doses of up to 240 mg in patients living with obesity or overweight and type 2 diabetes mellitus, in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Report initial Phase 1 study results for ACCG-2671 and initiate Phase 1 for ACCG-3535
catalysts.sentence: We expect to report initial Phase 1 study results for ACCG-2671 and to initiate a Phase 1 study for ACCG-3535 in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Topline data from body composition study and ACCESS OLE study
catalysts.sentence: Topline data from the study are expected in the second half of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Total addressable market for obesity more than $100 billion annually
tamClaims.sentence: This deeply rooted and global health crisis represents a total addressable market of more than $100 billion annually.
